message:
test(rch): cover ssh_error_code mapping + probe-summary classification (bd-nhrjr follow-up)

Follow-up test/polish pass for the previously-landed structured
`error_code` feature (bd-nhrjr, commit 1ae6af4), locking the
mapping of `SshError` to the shared `ErrorCode` catalog in place
and plugging a subtle double-count in the one-line probe summary.

### `rch/src/commands/helpers.rs`

  - New `test_ssh_error_code_mapping` exercises every `SshError`
    variant the helper handles and asserts it maps to the
    expected `ErrorCode`:
      * PermissionDenied              -> SshAuthFailed
      * ConnectionRefused             -> NetworkConnectionRefused
      * ConnectionTimeout             -> NetworkTimeout
      * HostKeyVerificationFailed     -> SshHostKeyError
      * KeyNotFound                   -> SshKeyError
      * KeyInsecurePermissions        -> SshKeyError
      * ConnectionFailed (generic)    -> SshConnectionFailed
    This pins the contract that `auto_recover.sh` and other
    downstream tools rely on — substring matching on the human
    `error` field is explicitly no longer necessary.

### `rch/src/commands/workers.rs`

  - Fix a double-count in the one-line probe summary: previously
    an unhealthy worker was reported *both* as "1 unhealthy"
    *and* in the `by_error_code` bucket as "1 RCH-E202", so a
    fleet of 1 unhealthy worker rendered as "9 worker(s) probed:
    0 healthy, 1 unhealthy, 1 RCH-E202." Drop the standalone
    `unhealthy` entry; `by_error_code` is the single source of
    truth. The behavior is documented in the function comment.
  - Align theme parameter type with the rest of the codebase:
    `rch_common::ui::RchTheme` -> `crate::ui::theme::Theme`
    (matches speedscore.rs and surrounding modules).
  - New `probe_summary_tests` module with three table-driven
    cases: healthy+error-code mix, uncategorized "other" bucket,
    empty input. Regression-guards the summary fix above and the
    deterministic BTreeMap ordering callers depend on.

### `rch/src/ui/probe.rs`

  - Two test fixtures constructing `WorkerProbeResult` gained
    `error_code: None` to match the struct shape that
    bd-nhrjr introduced. Test-only data plumbing, no behavior
    change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
